item.title: Faster Than Comcast? UNPOSSIBLE! Japan shatters internet speed record - 402 terabits per second (Tbps) over standard optical fiber
item.description: Researchers in Japan have clocked a mind-blowing 402 terabits per second (Tbps) over standard optical fiber, smashing the previous world record and leaving average U.S. broadband speeds (around 100 Mbps) in the dust—it’s roughly 4 million times faster. This speed could download the entire Netflix library in seconds or transfer 12,500 high-definition movies in a single tick. How they did it: Led by Japan’s National Institute of Information and Communications Technology (NICT), the team expanded bandwidth to 37.6 THz using multi-band wavelength division multiplexing and cutting-edge optical amplifiers. They transmitted data over 50 kilometers of commercially available fiber without fancy upgrades, proving it’s scalable for real-world networks. https://www.digitaltrends.com/computing/japan-shatters-internet-speed-record-can-download-entire-netflix-library-in-a-seconds/
item.dc:creator: Jbelkin

item.title: Mr. Rogers Stand Firm
item.description: Fred Rogers never lent his name to commercial products, so in 1985, when Burger King released a commercial featuring an actor dressed like him under the name “Mister Rodney,” he was deeply troubled. Though the ad was meant as light-hearted parody, Rogers feared it might confuse children who trusted and identified with his television persona. “To have someone who looks like me doing a commercial is very confusing for children,” he explained. He personally contacted Don Dempsey, Burger King’s Senior Vice President, who quickly agreed to pull the ad. Despite a $150,000 investment, the commercial only aired a handful of times before being removed. “Mister Rogers is one guy you don’t want to mess with,” Dempsey later admitted. “As beloved as he is, it just wasn’t worth it.” Rogers took misrepresentation seriously—especially when it risked misleading children or compromising the values he spent his life nurturing. When Johnny Carson spoofed him in a sketch titled “Mister Rambo’s Neighborhood” on The Tonight Show, Rogers voiced his disappointment. Carson responded with a public apology, acknowledging that the parody may have gone too far. Still, Rogers wasn’t opposed to all satire. He famously appreciated Eddie Murphy’s “Mister Robinson’s Neighborhood” on Saturday Night Live, a gritty, streetwise take on his own show. He understood the humor and recognized the sketch was aimed at an adult audience during a late-night slot, where children were unlikely to see it. But some uses of his image went too far. In 1998, Rogers was outraged when a Texas retailer began selling T-shirts featuring a doctored photo of him holding a gun, paired with the phrase, “Welcome to my ’hood.” This time, he wasn’t just disappointed—he was furious. Rather than simply requesting the shirts be pulled from shelves, he demanded that every copy be destroyed. Though he rarely displayed anger in public, Rogers was firm when it came to defending the principles he stood for. His influence extended well beyond children’s television. In the early 1980s, as the entertainment industry pushed back against home video recording, Rogers testified before the U.S. Supreme Court in support of VCRs. While many in television feared lost revenue, Rogers believed that allowing parents to record his show encouraged learning and brought families together. His calm, heartfelt testimony made a deep impression. The Court ruled in favor of home recording, citing Rogers’ remarks in their decision—a quiet but pivotal moment that helped shape the future of media, from VHS tapes to modern streaming. Though known for his gentle demeanor, Fred Rogers was never passive. He stood firm when it mattered—whether confronting corporations, challenging tasteless parodies, or influencing national policy. At the heart of it all was a steadfast commitment to children and their emotional well-being. His legacy is a powerful reminder that kindness is not weakness, and that quiet conviction can leave a lasting mark. As he always reminded his viewers, “You made this a special day just by being you. Credit: University of life
item.dc:creator: TheTaoofRob

item.title: Time to cut some onions
item.description:
Three years after Jorge went missing, Giorgi Berejiani's search ended in joy. a local business spotted a dog matching Jorge's description. Despite being tagged as a stray, Berejiani rushed to confirm it was his beloved pet. The reunion was emotional, with both Jorge and Giorgi overwhelmed with happiness.
The yellow ear tag on Jorge indicates he was captured as a stray by animal control in Tbilisi, Georgia, then vaccinated, sterilized, and released as part of a humane population management program. It's common for street dogs there to avoid repeated captures.
item.dc:creator: LogicDude

item.title: Bad week
item.description: What a week, but staying strong. Could use some power hugs tho. It was my birthday same-day that my brother's wife died, now it will always be the day that she died as well as my birthday. Maybe it's petty to think that way, but seems hard to loose that thought. Lots of negative thoughts, but staying sober is what keeps me going and gives me strength.

item.title: Today Phillies had a kiss cam and Phillie Phanatic joined in
item.description: Some people are saying it is just an affair why so much coverage, two consenting adults. So here's my take. It is NOT about the affair. This guy is a CEO and his ex-employees have accused him of bullying AND the woman in picture is head of HR. HR is known to snitch on employees they frown upon inter-office relationship. These two together makes it funny, ironic with a taste of schadenfreude

item.dc:creator: TheWhatever666

item.title: Oswald belongs to the universe now.
item.description:
Oswald crossed the rainbow bridge yesterday. He passed at home peacefully after a day doing all his favorite things. He was surrounded by love.
This is from the first week I had Oswald. He always looked a little worried like that.
Oswald was always a sweet and curmudgeonly sort. Never aggressive but mostly aloof. We would joke that he was running cat software on dog hardware.
We gave Ozzy his best last day. He was a scavenger hound (as evinced by other postings I've made here) and so we hid slices of pizza in the bushes for him to find.
This was from earlier in the month when we took Ozzy to the beach, one of his favorite places. Ozzy walked along side me before taking the lead and staring off towards the ocean. Ozzy is off to places I cannot follow him yet. He is no longer in any pain and he belongs to the universe now. And I hope he's on a grand adventure.
item.dc:creator: Paperhatguy

item.title: Strange Planet dump (from me)
item.description:
I haven’t posted a batch of these in a while
So I’m posting a big group of them
Also just saying hello. Hello Imgur
I have been busy because we have 2 kids now
And also I’m writing children’s books (see final images)
We also visited Australia for the first time and it was so fun
I also took some time to draw a map of the USA! Such a fun project
So as a parent my newer comics definitely do feature some new situations with offspring but also just…my tiredness
But life is good
And the kids are teaching me so much
They are 4 and 2 at the moment. We break up a lot of tiny fights
And we do step on a lot of toys
I’m collecting old experiences from previous decades,like the 90s - in order to show them someday
And of course showing them our favorite movies
We drink a lot of coffee
Oh and this is crucial, I figured out what kind of cookies my kids don’t like - it’s these thin ginger snaps
But seeing the world through the eyes of our kids is so hilarious
And teaching them how to play games is our favorite
Like teaching them how game deception is good
They find filth so easily
And make road trips hilariously long
They love my story ideas though, and they’re so encouraging
But again, we are falling asleep earlier than ever
After the daily chaos and mess
And normal anxieties of parenthood
When i hear them sing it’s the most amazing thing
And the 4 year old plays jokes on me now
So that’s all, just stopping by
Thanks for reading
So here’s the children’s book I finished. It comes out in September, so people are already preordering it
Here’s a sneak peek of the story
item.dc:creator: nathanwpyle2
